intro: Illipe malabaricum, commonly known as Illipe, is a tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. This plant is valued for its oil-rich seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Illipe malabaricum include illipein, illipe ester, and illipe oil, which are rich in fatty acids and have been shown to possess strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional medicine, Illipe is often used to prepare herbal ointments, creams, and salves, as well as extracts, which are used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema, acne, and wounds.
